content:
I shift my weight, feeling the familiar, stubborn resistance of the pavement. My boots are heavy with the grit of the morning, and I let that weight settle into my heels. I don’t need to look at Darnell to know he’s still twitching. He’s like that plastic bag—caught on something, but still trying to fly.

"You can't outrun the fog, son," I say, my voice as steady as the brickwork behind us. "It do
